similar_crash_fixes.subject: bugfix: avoid segmentation fault when Redcap tries to connect to gNB as NR_ServingCellConfig_t scd is NULL when Redcap does RA
similar_crash_fixes.author: Marius Tillner

similar_crash_fixes.subject: Fix segmentation fault when calling get_transformPrecoding function for BWP_id > 0 and rach_ConfigCommon = NULL
similar_crash_fixes.author: luis_pereira87

similar_crash_fixes.subject: fix a segfault when uci_01->harq is null
similar_crash_fixes.author: Mario Joa-Ng

similar_crash_fixes.subject: fix a crash (at this point "ue_context_p" is NULL,
similar_crash_fixes.author: Cedric Roux

similar_crash_fixes.subject: remove fixed size H_awgn_mimo that may cause segfault in simulator in case of more than 4 antennas
similar_crash_fixes.author: francescomani

suspected_functions.reason: Frame #0 crash point. The segmentation fault occurred inside this function at line 1065. It performs multiple pointer dereferences (e.g., accessing rrcSetupRequest fields, ue_context_p, UE pointers) and calls other functions without extensive NULL checks on critical pointers like ue_context_p and decoded cellGroupConfig. The function is complex and handles UE context creation and RRC setup message generation, making it the primary crash site.
suspected_functions.role: Called by crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - potential null/invalid call

suspected_functions.reason: Frame #1 immediate caller of rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest. It decodes the UL CCCH message and calls rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest with parameters derived from the decoded message. Potentially passes invalid or partially decoded data leading to crash downstream. It lacks explicit NULL checks on decoded messages before passing to the crash function.
suspected_functions.role: Calls crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - check parameter passing

suspected_functions.reason: Called by rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest to convert BIT_STRING to uint64_t. Contains a DevCheck macro that might not abort on invalid input, and no NULL pointer check on the input BIT_STRING_t pointer. If rrcSetupRequest->ue_Identity.choice.ng_5G_S_TMSI_Part1 is NULL or malformed, this function could cause invalid memory access.
suspected_functions.role: Called by crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - potential null/invalid call

suspected_functions.reason: Called by rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest to create a new UE context. If this function returns NULL or an invalid pointer, subsequent dereferences in rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest will cause a crash. The crash function assumes non-NULL ue_context_p without explicit checks after creation.
suspected_functions.role: Called by crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - potential null/invalid call

suspected_functions.reason: Called by rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest with UE pointer. It dereferences UE and UE->Srb pointers and calls other functions. Missing NULL checks on UE or UE->Srb could cause segmentation faults if UE context is invalid or incompletely initialized.
suspected_functions.role: Called by crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - potential null/invalid call

suspected_functions.reason: Called by rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest to generate and send RRC Setup message. It dereferences ue_context_p and UE pointers and calls multiple functions. Lack of NULL checks on ue_context_p or UE could propagate crash. Also uses buffers and calls external functions that might fail silently.
suspected_functions.role: Called by crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - potential null/invalid call

suspected_functions.reason: Called by rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest in error scenarios (not shown fully). It dereferences ue_context_p and UE pointers and sends RRCReject messages. Potentially involved if error handling paths are triggered but missing NULL checks could cause crashes.
suspected_functions.role: Called by crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - potential null/invalid call

suspected_functions.reason: Upstream caller in the RRC procedure flow that calls rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest indirectly or related functions. It manipulates UE context and configurations, potentially affecting UE state consistency and pointer validity leading to crashes downstream.
suspected_functions.role: Calls crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - check parameter passing

suspected_functions.reason: This function is the entry point for processing initial UL RRC messages and calls rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest. It is critical in the call chain and any malformed or incomplete message decoding here can cause invalid parameters to be passed downstream.
suspected_functions.role: Calls crash function rrc_handle_RRCSetupRequest - check parameter passing
